reasoning: Interest rates and bond yields generally follow a random walk, especially over short intervals such as 1 to 3 months, making the probability of an increase vs. a decrease roughly symmetric around 50%. The US is broadly expected to be in a very gradual rate-cutting cycle in late 2025, which gives real yields a slight downward pressure/drift over shorter forecasting horizons. Thus, I assign a probability just under 50% for the short-term resolution date. For the longer-term resolution dates, following standard forecasting rules, uncertainty dominates the short-term drift signal, bringing the probability closer to the baseline 50% random walk expectation.
